qa_pairs.question: What is LAMMPS?
qa_pairs.answer: LAMMPS is a classical molecular dynamics code.

qa_pairs.question: What does LAMMPS stand for?
qa_pairs.answer: LAMMPS stands for Large-scale Atomic / Molecular Massively Parallel Simulator.

qa_pairs.question: Who distributes LAMMPS?
qa_pairs.answer: LAMMPS is distributed by Sandia National Laboratories, a US Department of Energy laboratory.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find the project website for LAMMPS?
qa_pairs.answer: The project website for LAMMPS is http://lammps.sandia.gov/.

qa_pairs.question: Is there an online manual available for LAMMPS?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, an Online Manual is available at http://lammps.sandia.gov/doc/Manual.html.

qa_pairs.question: Where can users find the LAMMPS mailing list?
qa_pairs.answer: The LAMMPS mailing list is available at http://lammps.sandia.gov/mail.html.

qa_pairs.question: How is LAMMPS parallelized?
qa_pairs.answer: LAMMPS is parallelized with MPI and OpenMP.

qa_pairs.question: Can LAMMPS utilize GPUs?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, LAMMPS can run on GPUs.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find a list of all supported force fields in LAMMPS?
qa_pairs.answer: All supported force fields are listed on the LAMMPS package web site, classified by functional form (e.g., pairwise potentials, many-body potentials, etc.).

qa_pairs.question: Why is LAMMPS suitable for many areas of application?
qa_pairs.answer: LAMMPS is suitable for many areas of application due to the large number of supported force fields.

qa_pairs.question: Which force fields are suitable for modelling biomolecules in LAMMPS?
qa_pairs.answer: CHARMM, AMBER, OPLS, COMPASS (class 2), long-range Coulombics via PPPM, and point dipoles are suitable force fields for modelling biomolecules.

qa_pairs.question: What types of force fields are available for polymer modelling in LAMMPS?
qa_pairs.answer: For polymers, LAMMPS supports all-atom, united-atom, coarse-grain (bead-spring FENE), and bond-breaking force fields.

qa_pairs.question: Name some force fields used for materials modelling in LAMMPS.
qa_pairs.answer: EAM and MEAM for metals, Buckingham, Morse, Yukawa, Stillinger-Weber, Tersoff, EDIP, COMB, and SNAP are some force fields used for materials modelling.

qa_pairs.question: Which force fields does LAMMPS support for reaction simulations?
qa_pairs.answer: LAMMPS supports AI-REBO, REBO, ReaxFF, and eFF for reaction simulations.

qa_pairs.question: What are some force fields suitable for mesoscale modelling in LAMMPS?
qa_pairs.answer: Granular, DPD, Gay-Berne, colloidal, peri-dynamics, and DSMC are force fields suitable for mesoscale modelling in LAMMPS.

qa_pairs.question: Can different potentials be combined in LAMMPS?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, combinations of potentials can be used for hybrid systems in LAMMPS.

qa_pairs.question: Provide examples of hybrid systems that can be modelled using combined potentials in LAMMPS.
qa_pairs.answer: Examples of hybrid systems include water on metal, polymer/semiconductor interfaces, and colloids in solution.

qa_pairs.question: How can I check which versions of LAMMPS are installed on our systems?
qa_pairs.answer: You can check which versions of LAMMPS are installed on our systems by running `module spider lammps`.

qa_pairs.question: What is the format of LAMMPS version numbers?
qa_pairs.answer: LAMMPS version numbers are based on their release dates and have the format YYYYMMDD.

qa_pairs.question: What command should I use to view all installed LAMMPS releases?
qa_pairs.answer: You should run `module avail lammps` to see all the installed LAMMPS releases.

qa_pairs.question: How many modules are typically available for each LAMMPS release?
qa_pairs.answer: For each LAMMPS release installed, one or more modules are available.

qa_pairs.question: Give an example of different LAMMPS modules available for a single release.
qa_pairs.answer: For the 31 March 2017 release, examples include `lammps/20170331` (built with MPI), `lammps-omp/20170331` (built with USER-OMP support), and `lammps-user-intel/20170331` (built with USER-INTEL support).

qa_pairs.question: Do LAMMPS versions support GPUs?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, LAMMPS versions are also available with GPU support.

qa_pairs.question: What is the procedure to use a GPU-enabled LAMMPS version?
qa_pairs.answer: To use a GPU-enabled version, you must load the CUDA module before loading the LAMMPS module, for example, `$ module load cuda` then `$ module load lammps-omp/20170331`.

qa_pairs.question: What is the common executable name for LAMMPS on the clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: All prebuilt versions of LAMMPS on our clusters have a symbolic link called `lmp`, which allows you to execute LAMMPS by calling `lmp` regardless of the specific module.

qa_pairs.question: Does the LAMMPS executable name change between versions?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, the name of the executable may differ from one version to another, but a symbolic link called `lmp` is provided for consistency.

qa_pairs.question: How can I find the original executable name for a specific LAMMPS module?
qa_pairs.answer: To find the original name of the executable for a given module, you can load the module and then list the files in the `${EBROOTLAMMPS}/bin` directory.

qa_pairs.question: In the example provided, what is the original executable name for `lammps-omp/20170331`?
qa_pairs.answer: In the example, the original executable name for `lammps-omp/20170331` is `lmp_icc_openmpi`.

qa_pairs.question: Why do different modules exist for the same LAMMPS release?
qa_pairs.answer: Different modules exist for the same LAMMPS release due to variations in the packages included when the program was compiled.

qa_pairs.question: How many packages do recent LAMMPS versions typically contain?
qa_pairs.answer: Recent versions of LAMMPS contain about 60 different packages.

qa_pairs.question: Can all LAMMPS packages be enabled in a single executable?
qa_pairs.answer: No, not all packages can be enabled in a single executable.

qa_pairs.question: Where can I find documentation for LAMMPS packages?
qa_pairs.answer: All LAMMPS packages are documented on the official web page.

qa_pairs.question: What should I consider if my LAMMPS simulation fails with a particular module?
qa_pairs.answer: If your simulation does not work with one module, it may be related to the fact that a necessary package was not enabled when that module was compiled.
